3. Design the coil drain pan and trap:
# Drain pan
- Installed directly under each cooling coil (AHU or FCU).
- Must:
  - Be sloped to the drain outlet.
  - Have sufficient capacity for peak condensate + surge.
  - Be corrosion-resistant (galvanized steel, stainless, or approved plastic).
# Trap design (critical):
Traps prevent air from being sucked in or blown out of the AHU, which would block drainage.
- Blow-through AHU (fan before coil):
  - Positive pressure at coil.
  - Trap must be sized to prevent water being pushed out.
  - Recommended safety factor: ~½ inch water column
- Draw-through AHU (fan after coil):
  - Negative pressure at coil.
  - Trap must overcome suction so water can drain.
  - Typical trap depth: H + 1 inch (where H = static pressure in inches w.c. at coil)
Common mistakes:
- No trap or trap too short → water backs up, pan floods, air spray/mist.
- Shared trap for multiple units →

There are brands you admire from a distance and then there are brands that make you want to be in the room. boAt Lifestyle was always the second kind for me.
It felt alive in a way most brands spend years trying to manufacture.
So when the opportunity came to work with the team that actually built it, I didn't think twice.
Today, it means a lot to share that I'm now Chief Marketing Officer of this incredible company.
Getting here has taken a village though. Aman Gupta and Sameer Mehta for building a culture where creativity is taken seriously. Gaurav Nayyar for steering the ship, giving me the room to grow and make mistakes along the way. My team, who I learn from more than they probably realise and make my job look a lot easier than it is. And everyone who supported, shaped, or simply put up with me along the way.
